hypothesis_en: Mr President, apart from the fact that we would all like to enjoy eternal youth and, of course, the honourable Members of this Parliament, I am speaking clearly and seriously, I would first of all like to say that the first concern in the European Union with regard to the problem of pensioners is sustainability. In fact, within the reports on the economic reality of the different countries of the Union, it is an aspect that is systematically taken into account when analysing the situation. Secondly, the level of coverage and the level of balance and sustainability of pension systems and, as you know, in several countries of the Union reforms are being carried out to ensure the sustainability in time of these pension systems. If we make a presentation of priorities, we must necessarily concentrate on a few points, which are our proposals for action, but it is obvious that from what we talk about health quality to what is in the means of transport to facilitate for people who have limited mobility, to point out some clear examples Because, apart from the question of pensions, what we have to think about is that older people have to be fully integrated into society as a whole and that what cannot be marginalized, but on the contrary, included, integrated and always present.
reference_en: thank you Mr President (3.41) now of course (1.42) we'd all like to enjoy eternal youth (4.79) and surely members of the House too l= but joking aside the first responsibility of the European Union vis vis pensioners is to see to it that pension systems in EU states are remain sustainable and indeed in their reports on the e= economic realities in countries in the EU this is an issue that we are systematically monitoring and taking account of in effecting an analysis of the situation we look at debt and the deficit and growth but not just that we also ~consid the coverage the balance and sustainability of pension systems as th= as you will be aware reform is underway in a number of countries in the EU to guarantee the sustainability of pension systems over time secondly the (1.75) ageing of population of Europe well that's something that is taken account of in various different policies it's not a specific chapter or section because if we have a set of priorities we've got to focus on just a few points but very clearly (3.78) we talk for instance of issues such as quality healthcare or public transport which can be about people with limited mobility this is just to give you a couple of clear examples to indicate that that pensioners are at the heart of our concerns because talking about the question of pensions you must realise that above all what the elderly must be integrated in society they must be in no way marginalised or discriminated against they must be fully integrated and we must always be aware of their concerns thank you Mr President
source_es: muchas gracias señor Presidente a parte que todos aspiraríamos a gozar de la eterna juventud y desde luego los honorables miembros de este Parlamento claramente yo hablando ya en serio decir primero que la primera preocupación que hay en el ámbito de la Unión Europea respecto al problema de los pensionistas es la sostenibilidad de los sistemas de pensiones en los distintos países de la Unión que ése es el primer esfuerzo que se está reclamando y de hecho dentro de lo que son los informes sobre los distintos la realidad económica de los distintos países de la Unión es un aspecto que se está sistemáticamente tomando en cuenta a la hora de hacer el análisi= s= el análisis de la situación situación se además de analizar la deuda o el déficit o el crecimiento se contempla cuál es el nivel de cobertura y el nivel de equilibrio y de sostenibilidad en los sistemas de pensiones y como sabe su Señoría en varios países de la Unión se están llevando a cabo reformas para garantizar de durabilidad en el tiempo a estos sistemas de pensiones que es lo primero segunda cuestión en la cuestión de del envejecimiento de la población europea es algo que se tiene en cuenta en distintas políticas no aparece como un capítulo específico en porque si hacemos una presentación de prioridades hay que obligatoriamente concentrar en unos cuantos puntos lo que son nuestras nuestras propuestas de actuación pero es obvio que está desde en la la que hablamos de calidad sanitaria hasta que hay yo qué sé en los medios de transporte facilitar para personas que tienen limitada su capacidades de movimiento por señalar algunos de los ejemplos claros que hacen que ese tipo de políticas están presentes porque (1.19) al margen de la cuestión de las pensiones lo que hay que pensar es que las personas de edad lo que tienen que estar es plenamente integradas en el conjunto de la sociedad y que luego no se puede ni marginalizar sino al contrario ni ni discriminar sino al contrario incluir integrar y tener siempre presentes muchas gracias señor Presidente
